key_insight: Routine α-synuclein seed amplification assays (SAA) accurately detect α-synuclein pathology in memory-clinic patients and add information not captured by standard Alzheimer disease fluid biomarkers.
clinical_relevance: SAA may improve diagnostic stratification for Lewy body dementia and Alzheimer disease with co-pathology, supporting its use as a complementary routine biomarker.
method: 398 patients from the multicenter ALZAN memory clinic cohort were analyzed.
CSF and plasma biomarkers for amyloid, tau, GFAP, and NfL were measured alongside MMSE.
Synuclein status was determined using RT-QuIC seed amplification assay and compared with expert clinical diagnoses.
evidence_signal: In the cohort, SAA was positive in 95.0% of Lewy body dementia cases and 15.8% of Alzheimer disease cases, with high sensitivity and specificity for distinguishing LBD from non-LBD/non-AD patients.
watch_item: Findings are based on a memory-clinic cohort and do not show that current AD fluid biomarkers reliably identify α-synuclein co-pathology within Alzheimer disease.
paper_title: Routine implementation of α-synuclein Seed Amplification Assays reveals high diagnostic performance and the limited value of Alzheimer disease fluid biomarkers for detecting α-synuclein co-pathology
